steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure required
steps.content: The sentence reads: "they rest when they need ______ their food." The finite verb need takes a complement before the direct object their food. In Standard English, need requires a to-infinitive complement — not a bare verb, a future tense verb, or a gerund.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — digest: A bare infinitive (or present tense) cannot serve as a complement to need; the sentence lacks the required to. ✗
  • Choice B — will digest: A finite future-tense verb cannot follow need as a complement; two finite verbs cannot stack this way. ✗
  • Choice C — to digest: The to-infinitive is exactly what need requires as a complement, producing the grammatically correct "need to digest their food." ✓
  • Choice D — digesting: The gerund digesting does not function as a complement to need in Standard English; need digesting carries a passive meaning not intended here. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
